---
title: "Kiro CLI"
description: "AWS's agentic IDE terminal client, with MCP servers and optional headless mode."
---

<Note>
**Experimental pack.** Kiro CLI v2 is AWS's agentic terminal (formerly Amazon Q CLI). This pack installs kiro-cli v2+ and pre-installs common AWS MCP servers. v2's `--no-interactive` flag lets you run it headless with an API key — ideal for CI / scheduled jobs.
</Note>

## When to use

- Want **Kiro's agents + MCP servers** integrated out of the box
- Want headless mode for CI / automation (`kiro-cli --no-interactive "prompt"`)
- You're OK with an API key in Secrets Manager (or interactive SSO if you prefer)

## What the pack installs

- Kiro CLI v2+ via upstream installer (`cli.kiro.dev/install`)
- MCP server tooling: `uv` + `uvenv` + build tools (gcc, python3-devel)
- Common AWS MCP servers pre-installed:
  - `awslabs.terraform-mcp-server`
  - `awslabs.ecs-mcp-server`
  - `awslabs.eks-mcp-server`
  - `awslabs.core-mcp-server`
  - `awslabs.aws-documentation-mcp-server`
- (If `--from-secret` provided) `~/.kiro/env` (0600) with `KIRO_API_KEY`, sourced from `~/.bash_profile`

## Install — non-interactive

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Headless (recommended for automation)">
    ```bash
    # Store your Kiro key in Secrets Manager first (one-time)
    aws secretsmanager create-secret \
      --name /lowkey/kiro-api-key \
      --secret-string "$KIRO_API_KEY"

    # Then install — the secret ARN flows through CFN; raw key never leaves Secrets Manager
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack kiro-cli \
      --profile builder \
      --kiro-from-secret /lowkey/kiro-api-key
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Interactive (browser SSO after deploy)">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack kiro-cli \
      --profile builder
    ```
    Then SSM in and run `kiro-cli login --use-device-flow`.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Account assistant">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack kiro-cli \
      --profile account_assistant
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Pack parameters

| Param | Flag (top-level installer) | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `region` | `--region` | `us-east-1` | AWS region (informational — Kiro uses its own cloud) |
| `from-secret` | `--kiro-from-secret` | — | Secrets Manager id/arn whose `SecretString` is the Kiro API key |

<Note>
The top-level installer only supports `--kiro-from-secret` for headless auth. The pack-level script (`packs/kiro-cli/install.sh`) still accepts a legacy `--kiro-api-key` flag with a deprecation warning, but this is **not** threaded through the top-level installer, CFN, or Terraform. Always use `--kiro-from-secret`.
</Note>

## Resource requirements

| | All profiles |
|---|---|
| Instance | `t4g.medium` |
| Root volume | 40 GB |
| Data volume | 0 GB |

## First-run (post-install)

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Headless (API key already set)">
    ```bash
    aws ssm start-session --target <instance-id>
    kiro-cli --version
    kiro-cli --no-interactive "list running ECS tasks"
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Interactive SSO">
    ```bash
    aws ssm start-session --target <instance-id>
    kiro-cli login --use-device-flow
    # → follow device-flow URL in your browser
    kiro-cli
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## How `--kiro-from-secret` flows

```
install.sh CLI → CFN KiroFromSecret param → UserData env → bootstrap.sh → PACK_CONFIG
                                                                                │
                                        /home/ec2-user/.kiro/env (0600) ◄────── │
                                                                        pack install.sh
                                                                        resolves via
                                                                        aws secretsmanager
                                                                        get-secret-value
```

**The raw Kiro API key never appears in CFN state, Terraform state, UserData logs, or shell history.** Only the secret reference flows through deploy; IAM gates who can resolve it.

## MCP servers

Post-install, the pack's MCP servers are available inside the CLI:

```bash
kiro-cli
> /tools
# → lists awslabs.terraform-mcp-server, ecs-mcp-server, etc.
```

Add more via `uvenv install awslabs.<server>`.

## Tear-down

Delete the stack. `~/.kiro/env` disappears with the EBS volume. Rotate the Secrets Manager value separately if the key is shared.
